bullets.text: Full-year companywide CR of 88.8, the best in Progressive's history; Personal Lines CR 88.6; Commercial Lines CR 89.4; personal property CR 98.3 despite elevated cat activity.
Companywide NPW grew 21% YOY to $74 billion; Personal Lines NPW up 23%; personal auto drove growth at 22% PIF increase — highest organic growth rate in company history.
Total PIFs grew 18% YOY, adding over 5 million new policyholders in 2024; personal auto PIF growth of 22% represents the strongest PIF expansion in Progressive's history.
Board declared $4.50/share annual-variable dividend; $500M preferred shares redeemed; debt-to-total capital at 21.2%; investment portfolio returned 4.6% (equity 22.9%, fixed income 3.0%).
Advertising spend increased approximately 150% YOY as Progressive leaned into exceptionally strong unit economics to fuel growth; brand investment was a primary strategic lever in 2024.
Personal Lines vehicle non-acquisition expense ratio improved 0.4 points YOY; LAE ratio declined 0.5 points driven by lower frequency, higher average premiums, and technology productivity gains.
Profitability driven by sustained lower personal auto loss frequency; catastrophe activity elevated but manageable; personal property CR of 98.3 reflects cat weather pressure during the year.

Progressive earned Gallup Exceptional Workplace designation for the fourth consecutive year; employee culture cited as a key differentiator enabling the company's historic growth and profitability combination.
Continued rollout of auto pricing model 8.9, property models, and commercial Lines products; AutoQuote Explorer and Progressive Vehicle Protection expanded; Snapshot telematic program growing.
Personal auto industry broadly achieved rate adequacy in 2024 after years of aggressive rate increases; Progressive's early return to profitability (2023) gave it a significant head start on growth.
